name: Afghanistan
funFact: Polo on horseback was probably invented somewhere around here.

name: Åland Islands
funFact: Tiny Finnish islands where most people speak Swedish every day.

name: Albania
funFact: Nodding your head means 'no' and shaking it means 'yes' here.

name: Algeria
funFact: About 80% of this country is covered by the giant Sahara Desert.

name: American Samoa
funFact: Most kids here grow up climbing coconut palms.

name: Andorra
funFact: A tiny mountain country with no airport — you have to drive in!

name: Angola
funFact: Home to the giant sable antelope, found nowhere else on Earth.

name: Anguilla
funFact: People here race tiny wooden sailboats that they build by hand.

name: Antigua and Barbuda
funFact: It has 365 beaches — one for every day of the year!

name: Argentina
funFact: The tango dance was born in this country.

name: Armenia
funFact: One of the very first places in the world to make wine.

name: Aruba
funFact: It almost never rains here, even though it's an island.

name: Australia
funFact: Kangaroos can hop faster than a car drives in a town.

name: Austria
funFact: Mozart was born here and started writing music at age 5!

name: Azerbaijan
funFact: There are mountains here that shoot up actual fire from the ground.

name: Bahamas
funFact: There are pigs that swim in the ocean here — really!

name: Bahrain
funFact: It's a tiny country made of more than 30 islands joined by bridges.

name: Bangladesh
funFact: It's home to the Bengal tiger — one of the biggest cats on Earth.

name: Barbados
funFact: Singer Rihanna was born on this little Caribbean island!

name: Belarus
funFact: It's nicknamed 'the lungs of Europe' thanks to its huge ancient forest.

name: Belgium
funFact: Belgian fries (with mayo!) were invented here, not in France.

name: Belize
funFact: It has the world's second-biggest coral reef, full of sea turtles.

name: Benin
funFact: This is where the very first voodoo dolls came from.

name: Bermuda
funFact: Famous for the 'Bermuda Triangle' — but mostly for pink sand beaches.

name: Bhutan
funFact: This country measures success by happiness, not by money.

name: Bolivia
funFact: It has a desert made of salt so big it's seen from space.

name: Bosnia and Herzegovina
funFact: People here jump off a 500-year-old bridge for fun every summer.

name: Botswana
funFact: Half of the country is covered by the giant Kalahari Desert.

name: Brazil
funFact: The Amazon Rainforest makes about 20% of the world's oxygen.

name: British Indian Ocean Territory
funFact: Tiny coral atolls full of sea turtles and almost no people.

name: British Virgin Islands
funFact: Sixty islands, perfect for sailing pirate-style adventures.

name: Brunei
funFact: The king lives in the world's biggest palace, with 1,788 rooms.

name: Bulgaria
funFact: This country grows most of the rose petals used in fancy perfumes.

name: Burkina Faso
funFact: Its name means 'land of honest people' in two local languages.

name: Burundi
funFact: Kids here often herd long-horned cows that look like cartoons.

name: Cambodia
funFact: Has the world's biggest religious building — Angkor Wat temple.

name: Cameroon
funFact: Soccer is so popular here, kids play barefoot on dirt fields every day.

name: Canada
funFact: It has more lakes than the rest of the world combined!

name: Cape Verde
funFact: Ten green islands that rose out of the sea from old volcanoes.

name: Caribbean Netherlands
funFact: Three tiny tropical islands belonging to the Netherlands.

name: Cayman Islands
funFact: The water is so clear, you can see fish 30 meters below the surface.

name: Central African Republic
funFact: It has one of the world's biggest gatherings of forest elephants.

name: Chad
funFact: Has a lake that has shrunk to a tenth of its old size — sand keeps growing!

name: Chile
funFact: It has a desert where it didn't rain for 400 whole years.

name: China
funFact: The Great Wall is so long it would stretch across Australia.

name: Christmas Island
funFact: Every year, 50 million red crabs march across the roads to the sea.

name: Cocos (Keeling) Islands
funFact: Two dozen tiny islands shaped like a green ring in the sea.

name: Colombia
funFact: Home of bright pink river dolphins that swim in the Amazon.

name: Comoros
funFact: Tiny islands where ylang-ylang flowers go into fancy perfume.

name: Cook Islands
funFact: Children here learn to paddle traditional canoes before they can read.

name: Costa Rica
funFact: It has more kinds of butterflies than the United States and Europe combined.

name: Croatia
funFact: The necktie was invented here, by Croatian soldiers long ago.

name: Cuba
funFact: Vintage 1950s American cars still roll around its streets every day.

name: Curaçao
funFact: Famous for a bright blue liqueur named after the island.

name: Cyprus
funFact: Legend says the goddess Aphrodite was born from sea foam here.

name: Czechia
funFact: People here drink more beer per person than anywhere else in the world!

name: Denmark
funFact: LEGO bricks were invented here, in a tiny town called Billund.

name: Djibouti
funFact: It has a lake so salty you float on top without trying.

name: Dominica
funFact: Nicknamed 'the nature island' — almost the whole place is rainforest.

name: Dominican Republic
funFact: Baseball is so loved here, many MLB players come from this island.

name: DR Congo
funFact: Home of the okapi — half-zebra, half-giraffe, all mystery animal.

name: Ecuador
funFact: You can stand right on the equator — one foot in each half of Earth.

name: Egypt
funFact: The Great Pyramid is older than woolly mammoths going extinct.

name: El Salvador
funFact: It was the first country to make Bitcoin legal money.

name: Equatorial Guinea
funFact: The only country in Africa where Spanish is the main language.

name: Eritrea
funFact: Old Italian-style coffee shops are still everywhere in its capital.

name: Estonia
funFact: Skype, the video-call app, was invented in this little country.

name: Eswatini
funFact: One of the few countries left with a real king who rules.

name: Ethiopia
funFact: This is where coffee was first discovered, by a goat herder!

name: Falkland Islands
funFact: There are about 200 sheep for every single person who lives here!

name: Faroe Islands
funFact: Sheep outnumber people here, and the grass-roof houses are real.

name: Fiji
funFact: Made of more than 300 islands, but only about 100 have people on them.

name: Finland
funFact: It has more saunas than cars — almost one for every three people.

name: France
funFact: The Eiffel Tower can grow about 15 cm taller in summer heat.

name: French Guiana
funFact: Europe launches space rockets from a jungle base here.

name: French Polynesia
funFact: A hundred and eighteen islands and atolls scattered across the South Pacific.

name: French Southern and Antarctic Lands
funFact: A penguin's paradise — way down near Antarctica.

name: Gabon
funFact: Almost 90% of this country is covered in thick rainforest.

name: Gambia
funFact: A skinny little country wrapped almost completely around a long river.

name: Georgia
funFact: People here have been making wine for over 8,000 years!

name: Germany
funFact: More than 1,500 different kinds of sausage are made here!

name: Ghana
funFact: Home to Lake Volta, the biggest human-made lake in the world by area.

name: Gibraltar
funFact: Wild monkeys called Barbary macaques live on this giant rock!

name: Greece
funFact: The very first Olympic Games happened here almost 3,000 years ago!

name: Greenland
funFact: It's mostly covered in ice — the biggest island on the planet.

name: Grenada
funFact: Called 'The Spice Island' because it grows so much nutmeg and cinnamon.

name: Guadeloupe
funFact: A Caribbean island shaped exactly like a butterfly from the sky.

name: Guam
funFact: A small Pacific island where palm trees show up on the license plates.

name: Guatemala
funFact: Its quetzal bird's tail feathers can be longer than a baseball bat.

name: Guernsey
funFact: A tiny island that uses its own kind of pound coins.

name: Guinea
funFact: Bauxite from here ends up in soda cans all over the world.

name: Guinea-Bissau
funFact: Has 88 tiny islands, many with no people — just monkeys!

name: Guyana
funFact: Home to Kaieteur Falls, about five times taller than Niagara Falls.

name: Haiti
funFact: The very first country where slaves freed themselves and made a new nation.

name: Honduras
funFact: It once rained fish from the sky here — and people still talk about it.

name: Hong Kong
funFact: Its skyline has more skyscrapers than anywhere else on Earth.

name: Hungary
funFact: The Rubik's Cube was invented here in 1974!

name: Iceland
funFact: Has more than 100 volcanoes, and many of them are still active!

name: India
funFact: Chess was invented here a very, very long time ago.

name: Indonesia
funFact: Made up of more than 17,000 islands — count them if you can.

name: Iran
funFact: It's home to one of the oldest continually-lived-in cities in the world.

name: Iraq
funFact: Writing was invented here over 5,000 years ago — on clay tablets!

name: Ireland
funFact: It's nicknamed 'The Emerald Isle' because the grass is super green.

name: Isle of Man
funFact: Has a famous tailless cat breed called the Manx.

name: Israel
funFact: Has the saltiest sea on Earth — the Dead Sea, where you just float!

name: Italy
funFact: Pizza was invented in Naples over 200 years ago.

name: Ivory Coast
funFact: It grows the most cocoa beans on Earth — chocolate paradise!

name: Jamaica
funFact: Bob Marley, the king of reggae music, came from this island.

name: Japan
funFact: There are more vending machines than people in some neighborhoods.

name: Jersey
funFact: The cute 'Jersey' cow breed comes from this small island.

name: Jordan
funFact: A whole city was carved into pink rock here — called Petra.

name: Kazakhstan
funFact: The biggest country in the world that has no ocean coast.

name: Kenya
funFact: Lions, elephants, and giraffes all share giant grasslands called savannas.

name: Kiribati
funFact: One of the first countries to see each new sunrise on Earth.

name: Kosovo
funFact: One of the world's youngest countries — only declared independence in 2008.

name: Kuwait
funFact: It hardly ever rains here — sometimes only a few drops the whole year.

name: Kyrgyzstan
funFact: Most of this country is covered in tall, snowy mountains.

name: Laos
funFact: A landlocked country covered with elephants — friendly ones!

name: Latvia
funFact: People here celebrate the longest day of summer with bonfires all night.

name: Lebanon
funFact: Old cedar trees here are mentioned in the Bible — really, really old.

name: Lesotho
funFact: A whole country surrounded by another country (South Africa)!

name: Liberia
funFact: Founded by people from the United States who used to be slaves.

name: Libya
funFact: Most of the country is desert, and water comes from underground rivers.

name: Liechtenstein
funFact: One of the only countries with a prince who actually rules it!

name: Lithuania
funFact: It once had the biggest empire in all of Europe.

name: Luxembourg
funFact: This tiny country has the world's only Grand Duke as its ruler.

name: Madagascar
funFact: Lemurs live here and nowhere else on Earth.

name: Malawi
funFact: Has a giant freshwater lake full of more fish kinds than any other lake.

name: Malaysia
funFact: Two giant towers here used to be the tallest buildings in the world.

name: Maldives
funFact: Made of tiny coral islands, none of them more than 2 meters above the sea.

name: Mali
funFact: Has a city, Timbuktu, that used to be one of the world's richest places.

name: Malta
funFact: The whole country fits inside one fairly small city.

name: Marshall Islands
funFact: Has more lagoon area than land — water is everywhere.

name: Martinique
funFact: A tropical French island where everyone speaks French and Creole.

name: Mauritania
funFact: Has trains 2 km long carrying iron through the desert.

name: Mauritius
funFact: The dodo bird used to live here, before it went extinct.

name: Mayotte
funFact: A French island in the Indian Ocean with coral lagoons full of dolphins.

name: Mexico
funFact: Chocolate was invented here, by the ancient Mayans.

name: Micronesia
funFact: Tiny island nation where ancient stone 'money' can be bigger than a kid.

name: Moldova
funFact: One of the world's biggest underground wine cellars is here.

name: Monaco
funFact: The whole country is smaller than New York City's Central Park.

name: Mongolia
funFact: Has more horses than people, and many kids ride before they walk!

name: Montenegro
funFact: Its name means 'black mountain' — the mountains do look dark!

name: Montserrat
funFact: Half of this island is off-limits because of an active volcano!

name: Morocco
funFact: Has goats that climb up trees to eat fruit — really!

name: Mozambique
funFact: Its flag is the only one in the world with a real rifle drawn on it.

name: Myanmar
funFact: Has a giant golden temple covered in 2,000 diamonds.

name: Namibia
funFact: Has sand dunes as tall as skyscrapers in its desert.

name: Nauru
funFact: The third-smallest country on Earth — you can drive around it in 30 minutes.

name: Nepal
funFact: Mount Everest, the world's tallest mountain, is here.

name: Netherlands
funFact: People here own more bicycles than the country has people.

name: New Caledonia
funFact: A French Pacific island known for its rare blue-tongued lizards.

name: New Zealand
funFact: The kiwi, a chicken-sized bird that can't fly, lives only here.

name: Nicaragua
funFact: Has freshwater bull sharks living in a lake — yes, real sharks!

name: Niger
funFact: One of the hottest countries in the world, year-round.

name: Nigeria
funFact: The most people of any country in Africa — over 200 million!

name: Niue
funFact: One big coral rock island, with only about 1,600 people.

name: Norfolk Island
funFact: A tiny green island in the Pacific with its own special pine tree.

name: North Korea
funFact: People here use a different calendar that starts from 1912.

name: North Macedonia
funFact: Mother Teresa was born here, in the city of Skopje.

name: Northern Mariana Islands
funFact: Tropical islands where it's never colder than warm.

name: Norway
funFact: Some places here have sun all night for weeks every summer.

name: Oman
funFact: Has frankincense trees — the same kind mentioned in old stories.

name: Pakistan
funFact: Has the highest paved road in the world — way up in the Karakoram mountains.

name: Palau
funFact: It has a lake full of harmless jellyfish you can swim with.

name: Palestine
funFact: Olive trees here are some of the oldest in the world.

name: Panama
funFact: A canal here lets giant ships cut between two oceans.

name: Papua New Guinea
funFact: People here speak more than 800 different languages.

name: Paraguay
funFact: There are TWO official languages here, and almost everyone speaks both.

name: Peru
funFact: Llamas and alpacas roam here in the high Andes mountains.

name: Philippines
funFact: Made of more than 7,000 tropical islands.

name: Pitcairn Islands
funFact: Only about 50 people live here — one of the world's smallest groups.

name: Poland
funFact: Has a forest where every tree grows with a giant curve at the bottom.

name: Portugal
funFact: Sailors from this country mapped out much of the world long ago.

name: Puerto Rico
funFact: Has a bay that glows blue in the dark from tiny sea creatures.

name: Qatar
funFact: One of the hottest countries, but has malls big enough for indoor snow!

name: Republic of the Congo
funFact: Full of gorillas living in steamy jungles.

name: Réunion
funFact: A French island with one of the most active volcanoes on Earth.

name: Romania
funFact: Home of Bran Castle, which inspired the story of Dracula!

name: Russia
funFact: The biggest country on Earth — it spans 11 different time zones!

name: Rwanda
funFact: Half of all mountain gorillas in the world live here.

name: Saint Barthélemy
funFact: A tiny island once owned by Sweden, then bought by France!

name: Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha
funFact: One of the most remote islands on Earth — Napoleon was sent here.

name: Saint Kitts and Nevis
funFact: Two volcanic islands joined by a strip of beach.

name: Saint Lucia
funFact: Has two giant pointy mountains called the Pitons popping out of the sea.

name: Saint Martin
funFact: One island shared by France and the Netherlands at the same time.

name: Saint Pierre and Miquelon
funFact: A tiny chunk of France right next to Canada.

name: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
funFact: Thirty-something islands, many with no roads — only boats.

name: Samoa
funFact: People here can have tattoos that cover almost their whole body.

name: San Marino
funFact: One of the smallest and oldest countries in the world.

name: São Tomé and Príncipe
funFact: Two volcanic islands with cocoa farms everywhere.

name: Saudi Arabia
funFact: Most of it is covered by hot, sandy desert.

name: Senegal
funFact: Has giant baobab trees so big you can fit dozens of kids inside the trunk.

name: Serbia
funFact: Home of inventor Nikola Tesla.

name: Seychelles
funFact: Home to the world's heaviest seed — the giant coco de mer.

name: Sierra Leone
funFact: Diamonds are found in rivers here — sometimes just sitting in the riverbed.

name: Singapore
funFact: A whole country that fits inside one big city.

name: Sint Maarten
funFact: Planes land so close to the beach, hats blow off!

name: Slovakia
funFact: Has more castles per person than anywhere else in the world.

name: Slovenia
funFact: More than half of this country is covered by forests.

name: Solomon Islands
funFact: Has nearly 1,000 islands, many with their own language.

name: Somalia
funFact: Has the longest coastline of any African country — lots of beaches!

name: South Africa
funFact: It has three capital cities — not just one!

name: South Georgia
funFact: Penguin colonies here can have a million birds in one place.

name: South Korea
funFact: K-pop, smartphones, and tasty kimchi all come from here.

name: South Sudan
funFact: The world's youngest country — born in 2011.

name: Spain
funFact: People here have a famous tomato fight every August!

name: Sri Lanka
funFact: The world's first country to have a woman as prime minister.

name: Sudan
funFact: Has more pyramids than Egypt — about 200 of them!

name: Suriname
funFact: About 90% of this country is covered in untouched rainforest.

name: Svalbard and Jan Mayen
funFact: Polar bears here outnumber the people who live there.

name: Sweden
funFact: People here invented the seatbelt to keep families safe in cars.

name: Switzerland
funFact: Most of the world's chocolate companies started here.

name: Syria
funFact: Damascus, its capital, may be the world's oldest still-lived-in city.

name: Taiwan
funFact: Bubble tea, the chewy-pearl drink, was invented here.

name: Tajikistan
funFact: Mountains cover 93% of this country.

name: Tanzania
funFact: Mount Kilimanjaro, the tallest mountain in Africa, is here.

name: Thailand
funFact: White elephants are considered super lucky here.

name: Timor-Leste
funFact: One of the youngest countries in Asia — born in 2002.

name: Togo
funFact: A long, narrow country with markets full of colorful fabrics.

name: Tokelau
funFact: A tiny island country that runs on 100% solar power.

name: Tonga
funFact: One of the only Pacific kingdoms that was never ruled by Europe.

name: Trinidad and Tobago
funFact: Steel drums — those bouncy band drums — were invented here!

name: Tunisia
funFact: Many Star Wars desert scenes were filmed in this country!

name: Turkey
funFact: Has tulips that came here long before they reached the Netherlands.

name: Turkmenistan
funFact: Has a giant gas pit in the desert that has been on fire since 1971.

name: Turks and Caicos Islands
funFact: Forty tiny islands with some of the clearest water on Earth.

name: Tuvalu
funFact: One of the world's lowest countries — barely above the ocean.

name: Uganda
funFact: About half of all mountain gorillas in the world live here too.

name: Ukraine
funFact: Has the deepest metro station in the world — about 105 meters underground.

name: United Arab Emirates
funFact: Has the world's tallest building, the Burj Khalifa — over 800 meters high.

name: United Kingdom
funFact: Big Ben is the nickname of a giant bell, not the clock tower.

name: United States Minor Outlying Islands
funFact: Tiny specks of US land scattered across the Pacific Ocean.

name: United States of America
funFact: It has the world's longest cave system in Kentucky.

name: United States Virgin Islands
funFact: A US territory where people drive on the LEFT side of the road.

name: Uruguay
funFact: Almost all of this country's electricity comes from wind and water.

name: Uzbekistan
funFact: Has gorgeous blue-domed mosques along the old Silk Road.

name: Vanuatu
funFact: Eighty volcanic islands, some of which still erupt today.

name: Vatican City
funFact: The smallest country in the world — smaller than a stadium!

name: Venezuela
funFact: Angel Falls here is the tallest waterfall on Earth — almost a kilometer down.

name: Vietnam
funFact: People here zip around on more than 50 million motorbikes!

name: Wallis and Futuna
funFact: Three tiny French islands with three actual kings.

name: Western Sahara
funFact: A huge sandy area with more camels than people.

name: Yemen
funFact: Has buildings made of mud that are seven stories tall and 500 years old!

name: Zambia
funFact: Half of Victoria Falls — the biggest curtain of water on Earth — is here.

name: Zimbabwe
funFact: Home to ruins of an ancient stone city no one knows who built.
